turns.user_message: I used to get so excited about weekend mornings, waking up with a clear plan to just wander around the neighborhood or try a new recipe. Lately, though, I just stare at the ceiling and feel completely blank when the alarm goes off. It is strange how quickly everything shifted from feeling vibrant to just feeling heavy and dull. I keep trying to force myself into old routines hoping the spark will return, but nothing really lands anymore. It is just a quiet emptiness that sits right behind my eyes.

It has been over a month now and that initial blankness has settled into a steady routine of its own. I remember how easily I used to laugh at silly videos or lose track of time reading a good book, but now those things just feel like chores. Every day feels exactly the same, like I am moving through thick fog while everyone else is walking in clear air. I try to pick up my old hobbies just to prove I can still enjoy them, but my hands just go through the motions without any real feeling attached. The contrast between who I was a few weeks ago and who I am right now is honestly exhausting.

This heavy numbness has completely taken over my daily life and I honestly do not recognize myself anymore. I keep looking back at old photos where I am smiling so easily, wondering what actually changed inside my head to make everything feel so flat. Nothing brings me any comfort now, not even the simple things that used to ground me when I felt overwhelmed. It is like someone flipped a switch and drained all the color out of my world, leaving me to just exist in a gray haze. I wake up hoping today will finally be different, but the same hollow ache just greets me every single morning.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This just started recently.
